security.note: Aliases are configured for build-time resolution (e.g., via tsconfig.json or vite.config.js). Ensure aliases are restricted to project root and validated in build configuration to prevent path traversal. Implement the following mitigations: 1) Use absolute paths starting with '@/' to confine resolution within the project root. 2) Validate alias configurations in build tools (e.g., Vite, Webpack) to reject parent directory traversal patterns like '../'. 3) Consider using a security linter or pre-build script to audit path mappings.
